reasoning: A 1% increase from Wei Yi's October Elo rating of 2754 would require him to reach at least 2781.54 (essentially 2782). In the world of elite chess, gaining 28 Elo points for a 2750+ player is exceptionally difficult and rare, representing a performance rating well above 2850 over many games. Additionally, there are very few classical tournaments in the final months of the year that would provide the volume of games needed for such an aggressive rating climb. Given that the resolution dates are quite soon (Nov 2025 to Jan 2026), the probability of him jumping from 2754 to 2782+ is extremely low. Thus, probability estimates reflect this base-rate improbability, with slight increases for later dates as horizon uncertainty grows.
